title: Boot Sequence
description: The digital world loads like a webpage. The Calibrant's frustration is visible in the sky. Code scrolls under the party's skin.

lines.text: INITIALIZING... LOADING WORLD_05_DIGITAL.DAT... WARNING: PLAYER_PARTY EXCEEDS SYSTEM PARAMETERS
NOTE TO SELF: they are getting better at this faster than the curve predicted. The damage formula I rewrote for the Arbiter? One-shotted. FOURTEEN TIMES I rewrote it.
WELCOME TO THE SOURCE LAYER. Good luck. You'll need it. (just kidding. luck is a random seed. i control the seed.) (i think i still control the seed.)

lines: The world loaded around them like a webpage on a slow connection. First geometry — wireframe outlines. Then textures — green-on-black, terminal aesthetic. Then lighting — the cold blue glow of a monitor in a dark room.
Node Prime didn't look like a place. It looked like the idea of a place.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: We're actually in the code. Not fighting through a digital world. In it.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: We've been in the code since World 1. We just couldn't see the rendering.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Someone is watching us right now. Not through an NPC. Directly.

steps.text: In the sky, between streams of data: calibrant_frustration: ELEVATED.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Elevated. Not high. Not critical. That's the word of someone trying very hard to seem calm.
